title: Written In A Lady's Pocket-Book.
content:
Grant me, indulgent Heav'n, that I may live
        To see the miscreants feel the pains they give,
        Deal Freedom's sacred treasures free as air,
        Till slave and despot be but things which were.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "Written In A Lady's Pocket-Book.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
